Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 031 [Royal inscription of Assurbanipal][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x x TAR ŠU₂? NU?

(o 1') [...] ...

o 2'2'

[...]-⸢le⸣-e KU₃-MEŠ mim-ma x x [...] DU? ma-ḫar-ka kun-nu

(o 2') Pure [...], anything ... [...] ... established before you.

o 3'3'

[ia-a]-⸢ti⸣ mAN.ŠAR₂—DU₃—A MAN kuraš-šur⸢ki?⸣ [i]-⸢na⸣ mi-gir lib₃-bi-ki

(o 3') Me, Assurbanipal, king of Assyria, out of the kindness of your (fem.) heart1 [...] the just shepherdship which you (fem.sg.) bestowed (on me) at your (fem.) command.

o 4'4'

[...] x-ti₃ re-ʾu-u₂-⸢ti⸣ mi-šar ša₂ taš-ru-ki!(KU) ina qi₂-bi-ti-⸢ki⸣

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...]-⸢li⸣-šu₂ x TI.⸢LA⸣ ZIti₃ x x x x x

(r 1) His [...] ... preservation of life ...

r 22

[...] ⸢na⸣-mar ka-bat-ti x x x x TI x x x x x

(r 2) [...] brightening of mood ...

r 33

[...] x x MA? DU a-na ši-rik-ti šur-⸢ki⸣ SAR RA U? MAR? re-ṣu-ti

(r 3) Grant that [...] ... Come to help ... and stand at my side when I am killing my enemies. Establish the foundation of the throne of my kingship as firmly as a mountain for distant days.

r 44

⸢al⸣-ki-ma i-tap-pa-li i-da-a-a lu₂KUR₂-⸢ia⸣ ne₂-e-ri SUḪUŠ gišGU.ZA LUGALti-ia₂

r 55

⸢a⸣-na u₄-me SU₃-MEŠ ki-ma KURi šur-ši-di ⸢ma-nam⸣-ma ina LUGAL-MEŠ DUMU-MEŠe-[ia₂]

(r 5) When this attari-waggon becomes dilapidated and old, may somebody among the kings, [my] descendants, renew its dilapidated parts (and) put the inscription of my name with the inscription of his name. [...] will listen to his prayers.

r 66

[e]-nu-ma gišat-ta-ri šu-a-ti in-na-⸢ḫu⸣-ma DUku la-ba-⸢riš⸣

r 77

[an]-⸢ḫu⸣-ut-su ⸢lu⸣-ud-diš ši-ṭir šu-me-ia it-ti ši-ṭir MU-šu₂ liš-⸢kun⸣

r 88

[...] ⸢TI?⸣ ik-ri-bi-šu₂ i-šem-[me]1

r 99

[... a]-⸢šar⸣-šu₂ u₂-nak-ka-ru MU-šu₂ i-šaṭ-ṭa-[ru]

(r 9) [... who] changes its place (or) writes his name, may [...] not be present (at) his hand-lifting prayer. [...] ... [...]

r 1010

[...] ⸢ni⸣-ši ŠU-MIN-šu₂ a-a iz-⸢zi⸣-[zu]

r 1111

[...]-⸢iš⸣ [...]

(rest of reverse missing)

1ik-ri-bi-(erasure)-šu₂

1 Assurbanipal is addressing a goddess in this passage , as is clear from the feminine singular forms throughout.
